core_mechanism: mean-field statistical mechanics of a quenched-disordered (spin-glass) random Hamiltonian, yielding a phase diagram with order/disorder transitions and a glassy phase characterized by an exponentially large number of frozen metastable configurations (broken ergodicity)
brief_reason: The abstract states the method uses spin-glass theory to analyze a mean-field Hamiltonian with variable disorder, deriving a phase diagram whose spin-glass phase freezes the field's configurations into an exponentially large number of states.
